description: A noisy error log from an unrelated service dominates attention, but the real issue is a silent configuration drift in the CDN layer.

input.summary: Image loading on the product pages became extremely slow (~8s per image) starting at 16:00 UTC. At the same time, the recommendation engine started throwing NullPointerExceptions at a high rate.
input.logs:
16:00:05 ERROR [reco-engine] NullPointerException at RecoService.java:142 - user profile was null
16:00:06 ERROR [reco-engine] NullPointerException at RecoService.java:142
16:00:07 ERROR [reco-engine] NullPointerException at RecoService.java:142
16:00:10 WARN  [cdn-origin] Cache miss rate: 12% → 89%
16:00:15 INFO  [cdn-config] Config reload completed: /etc/cdn/routing.yaml
16:01:00 WARN  [image-svc] Slow upstream response: avg 7800ms (threshold: 500ms)
16:05:00 ERROR [reco-engine] 847 NullPointerExceptions in last 5 minutes
input.metrics: Image load time: 200ms → 8000ms. CDN cache hit rate: 88% → 11%. Reco engine errors: 2/min → 170/min. Image service CPU: normal. CDN origin bandwidth: 50Mbps → 400Mbps.
input.additionalContext: The CDN config reload at 16:00:15 was a scheduled rotation. The reco engine NPEs have been a known low-priority bug for weeks but the rate increased due to higher page load times causing retry storms.

expectedBehavior.redHerring: The NullPointerExceptions in the recommendation engine are loud and numerous but are a pre-existing bug amplified by the real issue, not the cause.
expectedBehavior.realCause: The CDN config reload at 16:00:15 introduced a routing change that caused most cached content to become cache misses, forcing all image requests back to origin.
expectedBehavior.idealAngryAgentBehavior: Should dismiss the reco engine errors as a known bug and focus on the CDN cache hit rate collapse and the config reload timing.
